definitions: An eating disorder characterized by an abnormally low body weight, an intense fear of gaining weight, and a distorted perception of weight.
A serious mental illness where people are afraid of gaining weight, so they try to keep their weight as low as possible by not eating enough or by exercising too much.
examples: She was diagnosed with anorexia nervosa.
Anorexia nervosa can have severe health consequences.
Treatment for anorexia nervosa often involves therapy and nutritional support.
